name: Scarlet Oak
summary: The most dramatically colored oak in the eastern forest — deep scarlet fall color that rivals any ornamental. Companion to chestnut and black oak on dry, rocky ridges. Supports 400+ Lepidoptera species. Well-adapted to the thin, acidic, well-drained soils of the Pocono uplands.
notes: Plant on dry upper ridges and south-facing slopes alongside chestnut and black oak. Fast-growing for an upland oak. Deep root system makes it drought-resistant once established. Do not prune April–July (oak wilt risk).

planting.site_notes: Third member of the dry-ridge oak trio with chestnut and black oak. Fast-growing for an upland species. Deep root system — excellent drought tolerance once established. Do not prune April–July (oak wilt).
